page.content: The table presents data categorized by headers: Numbers, Diagnosis, Age (y), Sex, PMI (h), and Tissue. In row 1, labeled "1," the diagnosis is Normal control, the age is 61 years, the sex is Male, the PMI is 21.00 hours, and the tissue is Motor cortex. Row 2, labeled "2," also indicates Normal control, with an age of 59 years, Male sex, a PMI of 10.00 hours, and Motor cortex tissue. Row 3, labeled "3," continues with Normal control, age 68 years, Male sex, PMI at 19.00 hours, and Motor cortex tissue. Row 4, labeled "4," identifies another Normal control, age 72 years, Female sex, PMI at 19.00 hours, with Motor cortex tissue. In row 5, labeled "5," the diagnosis remains Normal control, age 76 years, Female sex, a PMI of 3.00 hours, and Motor cortex tissue. Transitioning to ALS, row 6, labeled "6," reports an age of 59 years, Male sex, PMI at 6.00 hours, with Motor cortex tissue. Row 7, labeled "7," shows ALS diagnosis, age 70 years, Male sex, PMI at 14.00 hours, and Motor cortex tissue. Row 8, labeled "8," also falls under ALS, age 71 years, Female sex, PMI at 6.00 hours, with Motor cortex tissue. Row 9, labeled "9," indicates ALS, age 61 years, Female sex, a PMI of 22.00 hours, and Motor cortex tissue. Finally, row 10, labeled "10," records ALS diagnosis, age 87 years, Female sex, PMI at 18.00 hours, with Motor cortex tissue.
